armRank.description: Nullable Int64. Dense 1..n over THIS arm's evaluable, non-null-score population; descending by this arm's canonical score; ties broken by target_ensembl ascending. Null wherever that arm is not ranked. Reconstructible from the emitted table alone.

armScore.description: CANONICAL float64. Emitted, hashed AND ranked at full precision; display rounding is a UI concern only. NaN/inf are canonicalised to null. Null wherever that arm is not evaluable.

armSupportState.description: Within-dataset support FOR ONE ARM. Cell-level is deferred.

baseQcState.description: Pre-outcome, computed once, a function of NEITHER arm. In precedence order. A MISSING or INVALID required measurement is an explicit non-evaluable state, never a favourable one.

concordanceClass.description: DESCRIPTIVE ONLY. Never ranks, never gates.

contributorSource.description: The ONLY path to a guide identity. Null wherever identity was not resolved; a slot name is never evidence.

jointStatus.description: Derived from each arm's own evaluability and direction — NEVER from the tier. Both raw arm values ship alongside, so nothing this label merges is lost.

modulation.description: Derived per arm, only where that arm has direction evidence. The two arms are never collapsed into one drug direction.

modulationAgreement.description: A conflict is a FINDING, not a tie to be broken.

paretoTier.description: Nullable Int64. Frontier index: tier 1 is non-dominated, peel and repeat. Null wherever the target is not evaluable on BOTH arms — not tier 0, not last, not a sentinel. A tier is an ORDER, not a score: it has no units and cannot be averaged, and two targets in one tier are INCOMPARABLE, not tied.

projectionStatus.description: This arm's OWN projection outcome. The arms never share one.

replicationState.description: 'unavailable_no_contributor_evidence_in_this_release_pass' is the state of THIS release pass: the by-guide estimates carry no contributor evidence, so none was projected and there is no replication claim to make in EITHER direction. It is not the same as 'unavailable_unresolved_guides' (the guides were asked for and could not be resolved) and emphatically not the same as 'replicated_discordant' — reporting a negative result from numbers that were never evidence would be inventing one.

supportStatus.description: Support is INFERENTIAL, and AVAILABILITY is asked first. 'unavailable_no_contributor_evidence_in_this_release_pass' is the state of THIS release pass: the support estimates carry no contributor evidence, so support was never evaluated for ANY arm, however evaluable that arm is. 'evaluated' asserts that support evidence existed and was assessed, and may never be emitted where it was not. Where this is not 'evaluated', raw counts remain as diagnostics but no support boolean or tier may be true.

description: The ONLY schema a downstream lane (Perturb2State, Stage-3) may assume. There is no combined/balanced objective, no headline rank, and no A-only primary. A consumer MUST choose an arm explicitly. A target may be ranked in one arm, in both, or in neither.

hashes.description: Every binding that made this run. A consumer must re-check these, not trust them.

n_production_selectable.description: RE-DERIVED from the Stage-1 validation rows against the gate spec. No stored boolean is read. 0 means no production run is possible.

lane.description: production: gate-passed, Stage-3 eligible. research_only: same computation and same complete evidence, but the failed production gate is RECORDED not required; never production/Stage-3 eligible. synthetic: fixture only; never stands in for research.

production_gate_passed.description: the RE-DERIVED Stage-1 gate outcome, recorded even when it fails

question_id.description: Biology-only identifier (A, B, directions, condition). NEVER a run or cache key.

run_id.description: THE run key. Results live at <out_root>/<run_id>/. Never key a cache on question_id.

screen.description: screen.parquet, one row per SOURCE target, emitted in target_ensembl order (NOT by any arm — an arm-sorted emission order would be a headline rank).

arms.description: Two ordered, separate objectives. Never combined, averaged or reduced.

columns.description: The EXACT emitted column set: name -> pandas dtype. All 91 are REQUIRED and no other column may appear. Requiring a subset made the schema a floor, not a contract: a run could drop an arm's support status, replication state, tier or evaluability and still validate.

not.description: FATAL: a combined-objective alias (incl. balanced_score, composite_score, balanced_skew, balanced_a_to_b), a headline rank, a single eligibility flag, or any p/q column.

row_example.description: One fully-typed emitted row. Every emitted column is REQUIRED and every closed runtime vocabulary is enumerated here, so an enum that drifts in the code fails the schema instead of silently widening the contract.

not.description: FATAL: a combined-objective alias (incl. balanced_score, composite_score, balanced_skew, balanced_a_to_b), a headline rank, a single eligibility flag, or any p/q column.

title: Spot Stage-2 direct run — definitive two-arm contract
